reasoning: North Korea has been assessed as "prepared to carry out a nuclear test on short notice" since mid-2022, but has not conducted a test since September 2017. With only a two-month forecasting window remaining (May 1 to July 1, 2025), the base rate of a test occurring in this specific brief period is extremely low. While U.S. intelligence and South Korean authorities continue to issue warnings, similar predictions over the past three years (including before the 2024 U.S. elections) have failed to materialize. Although geopolitical tensions remain high and Kim Jong-un has called 2025 a "critical year," a nuclear test carries immense diplomatic risks, especially with China, and there are no signs pointing to an imminent test specifically scheduled for May or June. The probability over this short horizon is minimal.
